mission.title: Install and Configure Kserve on Kubernetes
mission.description: KServe is a standardized distributed generative and predictive AI inference platform for scalable, multi-framework deployment on Kubernetes. Installing KServe allows you to leverage advanced AI capabilities in your applications seamlessly.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KServe Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration. This allows you to install KServe using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add kserve https://charts.rock8s.com
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KServe CRDs
steps.description:
Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by KServe. This step is necessary for KServe to function correctly.
```bash
helm install kserve-crd-minimal kserve/kserve --version 1.0.1 --namespace kserve-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Deploy KServe
steps.description:
Deploy KServe using the Helm chart. This command will install KServe in the specified namespace.
```bash
helm install kserve kserve/kserve --version 1.0.1 --namespace kserve-system
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the KServe pods are running successfully. This command will show the status of the KServe pods.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for KServe to ensure it runs efficiently. Modify the deployment as needed.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kserve -n kserve-system
# Add resource limits under spec.template.spec.containers.resources
```

uninstall.title: Remove the KServe release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KServe release using Helm. This will remove the KServe deployment from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall kserve -n kserve-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove the KServe CRDs
uninstall.description:
Clean up the Custom Resource Definitions that were installed with KServe. This step ensures that no leftover resources remain.
```bash
helm uninstall kserve-crd-minimal -n kserve-system
```

uninstall.title: Delete the KServe namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the KServe namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kserve-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current KServe configuration and resources. This can be done by exporting the current state.
```bash
kubectl get all -n kserve-system -o yaml > kserve-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts available.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KServe
upgrade.description:
Upgrade KServe to the latest version available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm upgrade kserve kserve/kserve --version 1.0.1 --namespace kserve-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the KServe pods to ensure they are running the upgraded version.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If KServe pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability in the cluster. Use the following command to diagnose:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kserve-system
```

troubleshooting.title: KServe pods crash looping
troubleshooting.description:
If the KServe pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. Use the command below to view logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kserve-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the KServe service is not reachable, check the service configuration and endpoints:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kserve-system
kubectl describe svc <service-name> -n kserve-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KServe will result in the KServe pods running in the 'kserve-system' namespace with no errors. You should be able to access the KServe services as configured.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kserve https://charts.rock8s.com
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment kserve -n kserve-system
# Add resource limits under spec.template.spec.containers.resources

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
